config.title: Potsdamer Platz Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers for tourists deciding whether Potsdamer Platz belongs in a Berlin plan.

items.q: Is Potsdamer Platz Berlin worth visiting?
items.a: Yes, if you treat Potsdamer Platz Berlin as a **before-and-after history site** plus a modern architecture stop. It is less rewarding if you expect a pretty old square or make shopping the only goal.

items.q: How long do I need at Potsdamer Platz?
items.a: Plan **20 minutes** for the Wall trace and a quick look at the modern skyline, **45 minutes** for a comfortable loop, or **75 minutes** if you add Panoramapunkt, the Sony Center roof area or a Kulturforum extension.

items.q: Where is the Berlin Wall trace at Potsdamer Platz?
items.a: Look for the **metal strip and markers in the ground** around Potsdamer Platz and near the Deutsche Bahn Tower area. VisitBerlin also points visitors to QR-based time-travel markers around the square.

items.q: What should I not miss at Potsdamer Platz?
items.a: Do not miss the **Wall trace**, the contrast between Leipziger Platz and the modern high-rises, the Sony Center roof, and the view option from Panoramapunkt if the weather is clear.

items.q: Is Panoramapunkt at Potsdamer Platz worth it?
items.a: It can be worth it on a clear day if you want a quick high view of central Berlin. Check the official Panoramapunkt hours before going; the site listed daily opening and last-ascent times when this guide was prepared.

items.q: Can I combine Potsdamer Platz with a Berlin walking route?
items.a: Yes. It works well before or after Brandenburg Gate, the Holocaust Memorial, Topography of Terror, Checkpoint Charlie or a broader Berlin Wall walk. For the BerlinWalk core route, treat it as a separate add-on rather than the main 2 hours route.
